competitors.label: HTMLImageElement: srcset property
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical MDN reference for the srcset property and the browser-level responsive-image candidate selection behavior behind it.
theirStrengths: MDN is authoritative on srcset syntax, candidate descriptors, and the browser API surface for responsive image selection.
theirGaps: The API-style reference is less interview-shaped and less explicit about the production debugging failures caused by bad sizes values or mismatched asset widths.

realWorldUseCases: The `srcset` attribute gives the browser multiple image candidates so it can pick the right file for DPR and layout width. The production pitfall is shipping large images or mismatched `sizes`, which wastes bandwidth and makes responsive image debugging harder.
followUpConfusion: The common pitfall is providing srcset without accurate sizes or asset widths, which causes blurry images, oversized downloads, or confusing network debugging.

realWorldUseCases: MDN defines srcset accurately, but it is lighter on the concrete performance and debugging failures teams hit when sizes and asset widths drift out of sync.
actionableExamples: It includes direct syntax guidance and descriptor examples, so the example criterion stays competitive.
followUpConfusion: Our page is more explicit about the follow-up confusion developers usually hit next: why the browser picked a given candidate and why wrong sizes causes over-downloads or blur.

actionableExamples: Keep the practical scenario and DevTools note visible so the answer stays grounded in a real responsive-image debugging workflow.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical srcset reference, but our page is stronger as an interview answer because it turns responsive-image syntax into a concrete bandwidth, blur, and network-debugging decision.

competitors.label: Responsive images
competitors.selectionReason: web.dev has a direct responsive-images module that covers srcset, sizes, browser candidate choice, and real performance consequences for modern image delivery.
theirStrengths: It is strong on responsive-image theory and on practical candidate-selection behavior across viewport widths and pixel densities.
theirGaps: It is broader than the exact trivia question because it covers the wider responsive-images toolbox beyond srcset alone.

realWorldUseCases: The `srcset` attribute gives the browser multiple image candidates so it can pick the right file for DPR and layout width. The production pitfall is shipping large images or mismatched `sizes`, which wastes bandwidth and makes responsive image debugging harder.
followUpConfusion: The common pitfall is providing srcset without accurate sizes or asset widths, which causes blurry images, oversized downloads, or confusing network debugging.

realWorldUseCases: web.dev grounds srcset in real responsive-image behavior, but our answer is more compact about the exact production failures developers usually debug first.
actionableExamples: The tutorial includes strong responsive-image examples, so the example criterion stays tied.
followUpConfusion: Our page is clearer on the next question teams hit in practice: why the chosen candidate looks wrong in DevTools and which sizes or descriptor mismatch caused it.

actionableExamples: Keep the bandwidth-versus-quality trade-off visible so the page preserves one practical performance rule.

competitors.winReason: web.dev is strong on responsive images generally, but our page is better as a short interview answer because it packages srcset around exact DPR, sizes, and over-download debugging pitfalls.
